get.summary: Retrieves change request configuration for a project
get.description:
**Enterprise feature**

Given a projectId, this endpoint will retrieve change request configuration for the project

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: API key needed to access this API

put.summary: Updates change request configuration for an environment in the project
put.description:
**Enterprise feature**

This endpoint will change the change request configuration for a given environment, set it to either on/off and optionally configure the number of approvals needed. The minimum number of approvals is 1 and the maximum number is 10. If you provide a number higher than 10 or lower than 1, Unleash will clamp it to the allowed range.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: API key needed to access this API

post.summary: Create/Add change to a change request
post.description:
**Enterprise feature**

Given a change request exists, this endpoint will attempt to add a change to

an existing change request for the user. If a change request does not exist,

it will attempt to create one.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: API key needed to access this API

400.description: The request data does not match what we expect.

get.summary: Retrieves all change requests for a project
get.description:
**Enterprise feature**

This endpoint will retrieve all change requests regardless of status for a given project. There's an upper limit of last 300 change requests ordered by creation date.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: API key needed to access this API

get.summary: Retrieves number of project change requests in each state
get.description:
**Enterprise feature**

This endpoint will retrieve the number of project change requests that are: approved, applied, rejected, scheduled or awaiting review

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: API key needed to access this API

get.summary: Get scheduled change requests matching a query.
get.description:
**Enterprise feature**

This endpoint retrieves basic information about all scheduled change requests that match the search criteria provided in the query parameters. The endpoint will return any change request that matches one or more of the provided parameters. If you use no query parameters, you'll get an empty list.

For instance, to find all the scheduled change requests that either touch feature `MyFeature` or strategy `0D198067-7D55-460C-9EC7-DB86E3AE261A`, you would use the query string `feature=MyFeature&strategyId=0D198067-7D55-460C-9EC7-DB86E3AE261A`.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: API key needed to access this API

404.description: The requested resource was not found.

get.summary: Retrieves pending change requests in configured environments
get.description:
**Enterprise feature**

This endpoint will retrieve the pending change requests in the configured environments for the project, for the current user performing the request.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: API key needed to access this API

get.summary: Retrieves all pending change requests referencing a feature in the project
get.description:
**Enterprise feature**

This endpoint will retrieve all pending change requests (change requests with a status of Draft | In review | Approved) referencing the given feature flag name.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: API key needed to access this API

get.summary: Retrieves one change request by id
get.description:
**Enterprise feature**

This endpoint will retrieve one change request if it matches the provided id.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: API key needed to access this API

404.description: The requested resource was not found.

delete.summary: Deletes a change request by id
delete.description:
**Enterprise feature**

This endpoint will delete one change request if it matches the provided id.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: API key needed to access this API

put.summary: This endpoint will update the state of a change request
put.description:
**Enterprise feature**

This endpoint will update the state of a change request if the business rules allow it. The state can be one of the following: Draft, In review, Approved, Cancelled, Applied. In order to be approved, the change request must have at least one change and the number of approvals must be greater than or equal to the number of approvals required for the environment.

Once a change request has been approved, it can be applied. Once a change request has been applied, it cannot be changed. Once a change request has been cancelled, it cannot be changed. Any change to a change request in the state of Approved will result in the state being set to In Review and the number of approvals will be reset.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: API key needed to access this API

put.summary: This endpoint will update the custom title of a change request
put.description:
**Enterprise feature**

Change requests get a default title e.g. Change Request #1. This endpoint allows to make the title

more meaningful and describe the intent behind the Change Request

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: API key needed to access this API

put.summary: Edits a single change in a change request
put.description:
**Enterprise feature**

This endpoint will edit one change from a change request if it matches the provided id. The edit removes previous change and inserts a new one. You

should not rely on the changeId for subsequent edits and always check the most recent changeId.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: API key needed to access this API

404.description: The requested resource was not found.

delete.summary: Discards a change from a change request by change id
delete.description:
**Enterprise feature**

This endpoint will discard one change from a change request if it matches the provided id.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: API key needed to access this API

get.summary: This endpoint fetches the requested approvers of a change request
get.description:
**Enterprise feature**

Change requests can have requests for review from users. This endpoint fetches the list of requested reviewers for a change request.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: API key needed to access this API

put.summary: This endpoint will update the reviewers of a change request
put.description:
**Enterprise feature**

Change requests can have requests for review from users. This endpoint allows to add or remove reviewers from a change request. The maximum number of reviewers is 10.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: API key needed to access this API

get.description: Returns all configured [Context fields](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/unleash-context) that have been created.

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Whether the response should include project-specific or root context fields in addition to the fields in the default response. When querying the root context API, `include=project` will yield a response that includes all project-specific context fields in addition to all root context fields. Conversely, when querying a project-specific context API, using `include=root` will yield a response that includes all root context fields in addition to the project-specific context fields. The other combinations have no effect, because the responses already include those fields. When including project-specific context fields via the root-level API, context fields in private projects the user does not have access to will be omitted.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Find events by a free-text search query. The query will be matched against the event data payload (if any).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Filter by event ID using supported operators: IS, IS_ANY_OF.

parameters.name: groupId
parameters.description: Filter by group ID using supported operators: IS, IS_ANY_OF.

parameters.name: feature
parameters.description: Filter by feature name using supported operators: IS, IS_ANY_OF

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: Filter by projects ID using supported operators: IS, IS_ANY_OF.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter by event type using supported operators: IS, IS_ANY_OF.

parameters.name: createdBy
parameters.description: Filter by the ID of the event creator, using supported operators: IS, IS_ANY_OF.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: The starting date of the creation date range in IS:yyyy-MM-dd format

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: The ending date of the creation date range in IS:yyyy-MM-dd format

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The number of features to skip when returning a page. By default it is set to 0.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of feature environments to return in a page. By default it is set to 50. The maximum is 1000.

parameters.name: environment
parameters.description: Filter by environment name using supported operators: IS, IS_ANY_OF.

put.description:
Updates the lifetime configuration for the specified [feature flag type](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/feature-flags#feature-flag-types). The expected lifetime is an integer representing the number of days before Unleash marks a feature flag of that type as potentially stale. If set to `null` or `0`, then feature flags of that particular type will never be marked as potentially stale.

When a feature flag type's expected lifetime is changed, this will also cause any feature flags of this type to be reevaluated for potential staleness.

put.summary: Create (overwrite) variants for a feature in an environment
put.description:
This overwrites the current variants for the feature flag in the :featureName parameter for the :environment parameter.

The backend will validate the input for the following invariants:

* If there are variants, there needs to be at least one variant with `weightType: variable`

* The sum of the weights of variants with `weightType: fix` must be strictly less than 1000 (< 1000)

The backend will also distribute remaining weight up to 1000 after adding the variants with `weightType: fix` together amongst the variants of `weightType: variable`

get.description: Search and filter by selected fields.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: The search query for the feature name or tag

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: Id of the project where search and filter is performed. The project id can be specified with an operator. The supported operators are IS, IS_NOT, IS_ANY_OF, IS_NONE_OF.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: The state of the feature active/stale. The state can be specified with an operator. The supported operators are IS, IS_NOT, IS_ANY_OF, IS_NONE_OF.

parameters.name: lifecycle
parameters.description: The lifecycle stage of the feature. The stagee can be specified with an operator. The supported operators are IS, IS_NOT, IS_ANY_OF, IS_NONE_OF.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: The feature flag type to filter by. The type can be specified with an operator. The supported operators are IS, IS_NOT, IS_ANY_OF, IS_NONE_OF.

parameters.name: createdBy
parameters.description: The feature flag creator to filter by. The creators can be specified with an operator. The supported operators are IS, IS_NOT, IS_ANY_OF, IS_NONE_OF.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: The list of feature tags to filter by. Feature tag has to specify a type and a value joined with a colon.

parameters.name: segment
parameters.description: The list of segments with operators to filter by. The segment valid operators are INCLUDE, DO_NOT_INCLUDE, INCLUDE_ALL_OF, INCLUDE_ANY_OF, EXCLUDE_IF_ANY_OF, EXCLUDE_ALL.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: The list of feature environment status to filter by. Feature environment has to specify a name and a status joined with a colon.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The number of features to skip when returning a page. By default it is set to 0.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of feature environments to return in a page. By default it is set to 50.

parameters.name: sortBy
parameters.description: The field to sort the results by. By default it is set to "createdAt".

parameters.name: sortOrder
parameters.description: The sort order for the sortBy. By default it is det to "asc".

parameters.name: favoritesFirst
parameters.description: The flag to indicate if the favorite features should be returned first. By default it is set to false.

parameters.name: archived
parameters.description: Whether to get results for archived feature flags or active feature flags. If `IS:true`, Unleash will return only archived flags. If `IS:false`, it will return only active flags.

parameters.name: favorite
parameters.description: Filter by whether the feature is favorited by the current user. Use IS:true to show only favorites.

parameters.name: createdAt
parameters.description: The date the feature was created. The date can be specified with an operator. The supported operators are IS_BEFORE, IS_ON_OR_AFTER.

parameters.name: lastSeenAt
parameters.description: The date the feature was last seen from metrics. The date can be specified with an operator. The supported operators are IS_BEFORE, IS_ON_OR_AFTER.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: API key needed to access this API

401.description: Authorization information is missing or invalid. Provide a valid API token as the `authorization` header, e.g. `authorization:*.*.my-admin-token`.

403.description: The provided user credentials are valid, but the user does not have the necessary permissions to perform this operation

parameters.name: state

parameters.name: offset

parameters.name: limit

type.description: The type of the parameter. Corresponds roughly to [HTML `input` field types](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/HTML/Element/Input#input_types). Multi-line inut fields are indicated as `textfield` (equivalent to the HTML `textarea` tag).

required.description: Whether this parameter is required or not. If a parameter is required, you must give it a value when you create the addon. If it is not required it can be left out. It may receive a default value in those cases.

sensitive.description: Indicates whether this parameter is **sensitive** or not. Unleash will not return sensitive parameters to API requests. It will instead use a number of asterisks to indicate that a value is set, e.g. "******". The number of asterisks does not correlate to the parameter's value.

provider.description:
The addon provider, such as "webhook" or "slack". This string is **case sensitive** and maps to the provider's `name` property.

The list of all supported providers and their parameters for a specific Unleash instance can be found by making a GET request to the `api/admin/addons` endpoint: the `providers` property of that response will contain all available providers.

The default set of providers can be found in the [addons reference documentation](https://docs.getunleash.io/integrate). The default supported options are:
- `datadog` for [Datadog](https://docs.getunleash.io/integrate/datadog)
- `slack` for [Slack](https://docs.getunleash.io/integrate/slack)
- `teams` for [Microsoft Teams](https://docs.getunleash.io/integrate/teams)
- `webhook` for [webhooks](https://docs.getunleash.io/integrate/webhook)

The provider you choose for your addon dictates what properties the `parameters` object needs. Refer to the documentation for each provider for more information.

enabled.description: Whether to enable or disable OpenID Connect for this instance

autoCreate.description: Auto create users based on email addresses from login tokens

acrValues.description:
Authentication Context Class Reference, used to request extra values in the acr claim returned from the server. If multiple values are required, they should be space separated.

addGroupsScope.description: When enabled Unleash will also request the 'groups' scope as part of the login request.

enablePkce.description: Enable PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange) for enhanced security. Recommended for public clients and provides additional protection against authorization code interception attacks.

id.description: The banner's ID. Banner IDs are incrementing integers. In other words, a more recently created banner will always have a higher ID than an older one.

message.description: The message to display to all users. Supports markdown.

enabled.description: Whether the banner should be displayed currently. If not specified, defaults to true.

variant.description: The variant of the banner. One of "info", "warning", "error", or "success". If not specified, defaults to "info".

sticky.description: Whether the banner should be sticky on the screen. If not specified, defaults to `false`.

icon.description: The icon to display on the banner. Can be one of https://fonts.google.com/icons. If not specified, this will be the default icon for the variant. If "none", no icon will be displayed.

link.description: The link to display on the banner. Can either be an absolute or a relative link (e.g. absolute: "https://example.com" or relative: "/admin/service-accounts"). If "dialog", will display a dialog when clicked. If not specified, no link will be displayed.

linkText.description: The text to display on the link. If not specified, will be displayed as "More info".

dialogTitle.description: The title to display on the dialog. If not specified, this will be the same as `linkText`.

dialog.description: The markdown to display on the dialog. If not specified, no dialog will be displayed.

createdAt.description: The date and time of when the banner was created.

bannerSchema.description: An object describing a banner to be displayed to all users.
bannerSchema.title: bannerSchema

bannersSchema.description: A response model with a list of banners.
bannersSchema.title: bannersSchema

name.description: The name of the error kind

message.description: A description of what went wrong.
The message to display to all users. Supports markdown.

enabled.description: Whether the banner should be displayed currently. If not specified, defaults to true.

variant.description: The variant of the banner. One of "info", "warning", "error", or "success". If not specified, defaults to "info".

sticky.description: Whether the banner should be sticky on the screen. If not specified, defaults to `false`.

icon.description: The icon to display on the banner. Can be one of https://fonts.google.com/icons. If not specified, this will be the default icon for the variant. If "none", no icon will be displayed.

link.description: The link to display on the banner. Can either be an absolute or a relative link (e.g. absolute: "https://example.com" or relative: "/admin/service-accounts"). If "dialog", will display a dialog when clicked. If not specified, no link will be displayed.

linkText.description: The text to display on the link. If not specified, will be displayed as "More info".

dialogTitle.description: The title to display on the dialog. If not specified, this will be the same as `linkText`.

dialog.description: The markdown to display on the dialog. If not specified, no dialog will be displayed.

The [type of environment](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/environments#environment-types) you would like to create. Unleash officially recognizes the following values:
- `development`
- `test`
- `preproduction`
- `production`

If you pass a string that is not one of the recognized values, Unleash will accept it, but it will carry no special semantics.

AdvancedPlaygroundEnvironmentFeatureSche.description:
The cumulative results of all the feature's strategies. Can be `true`,
                                  `false`, or `unknown`.
                                  This property will only be `unknown`
                                  if one or more of the strategies can't be fully evaluated and the rest of the strategies
                                  all resolve to `false`.
AdvancedPlaygroundEnvironmentFeatureSche.title: AdvancedPlaygroundEnvironmentFeatureSchemaStrategiesResult

PlaygroundStrategySchemaResultOneOf0Eval.description: Signals that this strategy could not be evaluated. This is most likely because you're using a custom strategy that Unleash doesn't know about. The `unevaluated` result is also returned if the strategy is disabled.
PlaygroundStrategySchemaResultOneOf0Eval.title: PlaygroundStrategySchemaResultOneOf0EvaluationStatus

PlaygroundStrategySchemaResultOneOf0Enab.description: Whether this strategy resolves to `false` or if it might resolve to `true`. Because Unleash can't evaluate the strategy, it can't say for certain whether it will be `true`, but if you have failing constraints or segments, it _can_ determine that your strategy would be `false`.
PlaygroundStrategySchemaResultOneOf0Enab.title: PlaygroundStrategySchemaResultOneOf0Enabled

evaluationStatus.description: Signals that this strategy could not be evaluated. This is most likely because you're using a custom strategy that Unleash doesn't know about. The `unevaluated` result is also returned if the strategy is disabled.

enabled.description: Whether this strategy resolves to `false` or if it might resolve to `true`. Because Unleash can't evaluate the strategy, it can't say for certain whether it will be `true`, but if you have failing constraints or segments, it _can_ determine that your strategy would be `false`.

PlaygroundStrategySchemaResultOneOf1Eval.description: Signals that this strategy was evaluated successfully.
PlaygroundStrategySchemaResultOneOf1Eval.title: PlaygroundStrategySchemaResultOneOf1EvaluationStatus

type.description: The format of the payload.

PlaygroundStrategySchemaResultOneOf1Vari.description: An optional payload attached to the variant.
PlaygroundStrategySchemaResultOneOf1Vari.title: PlaygroundStrategySchemaResultOneOf1VariantPayload

name.description: The variant's name. If there is no variant or if the toggle is disabled, this will be `disabled`

enabled.description: Whether the variant is enabled or not. If the feature is disabled or if it doesn't have variants, this property will be `false`

payload.description: An optional payload attached to the variant.

PlaygroundStrategySchemaResultOneOf1Vari.description:
The feature variant you receive based on the provided context or the _disabled
                          variant_. If a feature is disabled or doesn't have any
                          variants, you would get the _disabled variant_.
                          Otherwise, you'll get one of the feature's defined variants.
PlaygroundStrategySchemaResultOneOf1Vari.title: PlaygroundStrategySchemaResultOneOf1Variant

evaluationStatus.description: Signals that this strategy was evaluated successfully.

enabled.description: Whether this strategy evaluates to true or not.

variant.description:
The feature variant you receive based on the provided context or the _disabled
                          variant_. If a feature is disabled or doesn't have any
                          variants, you would get the _disabled variant_.
                          Otherwise, you'll get one of the feature's defined variants.

variants.description: The feature variants.

PlaygroundStrategySchemaResult.description: The strategy's evaluation result. If the strategy is a custom strategy that Unleash can't evaluate, `evaluationStatus` will be `unknown`. Otherwise, it will be `true` or `false`
PlaygroundStrategySchemaResult.title: PlaygroundStrategySchemaResult

PlaygroundConstraintSchemaOperator.description: The operator to use when evaluating this constraint. For more information about the various operators, refer to [the strategy constraint operator documentation](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/activation-strategies#constraint-operators).
PlaygroundConstraintSchemaOperator.title: PlaygroundConstraintSchemaOperator

contextName.description: The name of the context field that this constraint should apply to.

operator.description: The operator to use when evaluating this constraint. For more information about the various operators, refer to [the strategy constraint operator documentation](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/activation-strategies#constraint-operators).

caseInsensitive.description: Whether the operator should be case sensitive or not. Defaults to `false` (being case sensitive).

inverted.description: Whether the result should be negated or not. If `true`, will turn a `true` result into a `false` result and vice versa.

values.description: The context values that should be used for constraint evaluation. Use this property instead of `value` for properties that accept multiple values.

value.description: The context value that should be used for constraint evaluation. Use this property instead of `values` for properties that only accept single values.

result.description: Whether this was evaluated as true or false.

playgroundConstraintSchema.description: A strategy constraint. For more information, refer to [the strategy constraint reference documentation](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/activation-strategies#constraints)
playgroundConstraintSchema.title: playgroundConstraintSchema

id.description: The segment's id.

name.description: The name of the segment.

result.description: Whether this was evaluated as true or false.

constraints.description: The list of constraints in this segment.

playgroundSegmentSchema.description: The evaluated result of a segment as used by the Playground.
playgroundSegmentSchema.title: playgroundSegmentSchema

PlaygroundStrategySchemaLinks.description: A set of links to actions you can perform on this strategy
PlaygroundStrategySchemaLinks.title: PlaygroundStrategySchemaLinks

name.description: The strategy's name.

title.description: Description of the feature's purpose.

id.description: The strategy's id.

result.description: The strategy's evaluation result. If the strategy is a custom strategy that Unleash can't evaluate, `evaluationStatus` will be `unknown`. Otherwise, it will be `true` or `false`

Statistics for a project, including the average time to production, number of features created, the project activity and more.

EdgeTokenSchemaType.description: The [API token](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/api-tokens-and-client-keys)'s **type**. Unleash supports three different types of API tokens ([ADMIN](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/api-tokens-and-client-keys#admin-tokens), [CLIENT](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/api-tokens-and-client-keys#backend-tokens), [FRONTEND](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/api-tokens-and-client-keys#frontend-tokens)). They all have varying access, so when validating a token it's important to know what kind you're dealing with
EdgeTokenSchemaType.title: EdgeTokenSchemaType

type.description: The [API token](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/api-tokens-and-client-keys)'s **type**. Unleash supports three different types of API tokens ([ADMIN](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/api-tokens-and-client-keys#admin-tokens), [CLIENT](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/api-tokens-and-client-keys#backend-tokens), [FRONTEND](https://docs.getunleash.io/concepts/api-tokens-and-client-keys#frontend-tokens)). They all have varying access, so when validating a token it's important to know what kind you're dealing with

edgeTokenSchema.title: edgeTokenSchema
